replacement_target: later explicit post-baseline AIDE roadmap checkpoint or approved replacement closure checkpoint only

not_doing: scope reopening without explicit follow-up checkpoint

family_completion_findings.summary: Canonical XStack meaning, scope buckets, in-scope and out-of-scope boundaries, and Dominium-retained posture were frozen.
A detailed live-surface ledger exists, but some surfaces remain intentionally mixed, legacy, or later-review only.
The portable task-unit shape was frozen without adding runtime or scheduler semantics.
Portable evidence and review semantics were frozen with explicit refusal, rejection, acknowledgement, approval, and escalation distinctions.
Portable policy and permission declarations were frozen while enforcement/runtime machinery stayed retained or deferred.
Portable capability-profile declaration shape and its contract links were frozen without creating a resolver/runtime.
The portable adapter declaration was frozen without implementing adapter machinery.
One canonical build/validation/playtest/session rule set was frozen, but it honestly preserves current baseline fragility and blockers.
The extraction-boundary ledger and staged post-baseline order were frozen, with mixed and baseline-critical surfaces explicitly deferred.

portable_now_summary.meaning: Portable now means semantically frozen and reusable at the document-first or contract-first layer, not already extracted into a separate runtime or repo.
portable_document_first_assets: XStack meaning and scope buckets from X-0
inventory and classification fact base from X-1

retained_surfaces: session creation, boot, runtime control, and save-root-sensitive paths
AppShell, launcher, setup, and operator-command surfaces
local loopback authority, local singleplayer bridge, and baseline runtime glue
pack, profile, lock, registry-compile, and packaging/distribution pipelines
release/trust consumers and repo-specific verification/publication behavior
validation/reporting surfaces that still own repo suites, report sinks, or Xi-specific policy
the Codex repo operating contract itself as a Dominium repo-law surface

deferred_until_post_baseline: daemon, scheduler, worker-pool, compiler, bakeoff, or adapter-runtime machinery
broad wrapper or bridge implementation across the repo
mixed-surface review for UI bind, validation kernels, CI guardrail policy, and gate/review bridge layers
any extraction or promotion of session/runtime/AppShell/launcher/setup/local-loopback/release-trust paths
any reopening of XStack meaning, scope buckets, or portable contract shapes without an explicit follow-up checkpoint

immediate_operational_value.value: Future Codex runs now have one explicit XStack/AIDE packet plus one repo-operating contract instead of inferring scope and ownership from drifted docs.
Prompts can separate portable-contract work from Dominium runtime work cleanly and reject overbroad AIDE asks earlier.
Protected, mixed, retained, deferred, and out-of-scope surfaces are now explicit instead of being rediscovered per run.
Later AIDE work can start from a staged extraction map with prerequisites and shim requirements instead of reopening repo-wide portability debate.
The series now acts as a constraint packet behind baseline assembly rather than as a competing architecture or platform track.

next_step_after_baseline.purpose: Re-check the actual playable baseline against the operating contract and extraction map, confirm which extraction prerequisites are now satisfied, and admit or refuse a first post-baseline extraction-review tranche.
should_consume: fresh post-baseline validation and smoke evidence
must_not_do: broad code extraction without a later admission review

forbidden_followups: using closure to justify immediate AIDE platformization
reopening the XStack scope freeze because a surface sounds generic
extracting baseline-critical Dominium surfaces before the local playable baseline exists
treating contract freezes as proof that runtime implementation is complete
using the extraction map to justify broad renames, code movement, or repo splits now
creating new XStack/AIDE prompts before the baseline that compete with startup/session/playtest hardening
treating the repo-operating contract as AIDE operating policy instead of Dominium repo law

final_verdict.phase_completion_meaning: The intended narrowing, contract-freeze, repo-operating, and extraction-boundary mission is complete.
